mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the equity discount rate and compresses the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets and strengthening consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 3 months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and payroll employment indicates a self-reinforcing demand expansion where income growth fuels consumption, amplifying earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of RSAFS and PAYEMS in top tercile fail to predict positive 60d returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market recoveries, sustained high levels of housing starts signal durable goods demand and construction sector profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST > 1200 coincides with negative 60d SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates improving factory utilization and operating leverage, which expands profit margins and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration does not coincide with positive 5d SPX returns in > 55% of weeks over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index increases exceeding 0.5 percent over two months signal eroding real corporate earnings and expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.5% in 2 months coincides with positive 20d SPX returns in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with elevated federal funds rates creates a policy-constrained demand environment where consumer strength invites further tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: High RSAFS combined with high FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 5d returns with Rank IC < -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a sequential GDP contraction signals an impending demand cliff that reduces aggregate corporate revenue visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contraction in late-cycle expansions coincides with positive 60d SPX returns in > 50% of instances.
